# Mitochondria

Mitochondria are compartments inside most of our cells that produce the bulk of the
cell's usable energy. They convert nutrients and oxygen into a molecule called ATP,
which powers almost everything a cell does. Tissues that demand a lot of energy, such
as muscle and the brain, are especially rich in mitochondria.

## Their own DNA

Mitochondria carry a small loop of their own DNA, separate from the DNA in the cell
nucleus. This and other clues suggest they descend from free-living bacteria that
were engulfed by an ancestral cell long ago and became permanent partners. In humans,
mitochondrial DNA is inherited almost entirely from the mother, which makes it useful
for tracing maternal ancestry.
